matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes secondary school mathematics students cube cuboid (rectangular prism) right circular cylinder right circular cone sphere hemisphere surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic mathematics: surface areas & volumes what we will achieve today learning goals for 3-d shapes identify properties of 3-d shapes (radius, height, base). apply correct formulas for surface area and volume. solve word problems involving capacity and covering. shape properties & definitions definitions of cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere, hemisphere cube: all sides equal (a). cuboid: length, width, height (l, b, h). cylinder/cone/sphere/hemisphere share radius (r) and height (h). total surface area (cube & cuboid) tsa of cube and cuboid cube tsa = 6a² cuboid tsa = 2(lb + bh + hl) lateral surface area excludes top and bottom. curved surfaces (cylinder & cone) tsa and csa of cylinder and cone cylinder: 2πr(h + r) cone: πr(l + r) slant height 'l' vs vertical height 'h'. hemisphere tsa = 3πr² calculating volume (capacity) volume formulas for all shapes cube: a³ cuboid: l×b×h cylinder/cone/sphere/hemisphere formulas. example calculation steps step-by...
